name: Humble Pie
description: '''Humble Pie''' are an English [[Rock music|rock]] band formed by former [[Small Faces]] vocalist and guitarist [[Steve Marriott]] and former [[The Herd (British band)|Herd]] vocalist and guitarist [[Peter Frampton]] in Moreton, Essex, in 1969. The original line-up was completed by former [[Spooky Tooth]] bassist [[Greg Ridley]] and former [[Apostolic Intervention]] drummer [[Jerry Shirley]]. They are known as one of the first [[Supergroup (music)|supergroups]] of the late 1960s and enjoyed success in the early 1970s with songs such as "[[Black Coffee (Ike & Tina Turner song)|Black Coffee]]", "[[30 Days in the Hole]]", "[[I Don't Need No Doctor]]", "[[Hot 'n' Nasty]]" and "[[Natural Born Bugie]]". Frampton left the band in 1971 and was replaced by [[Clem Clempson]].
